title: OOF multi-family blend with rank-preserving calibration
summary: The strongest public tabular practice here is usually heterogeneous ensembling, but calibration must respect AUC.
rationale: The strongest public tabular practice here is usually heterogeneous ensembling, but calibration must respect AUC. Scikit-learn notes sigmoid calibration is strictly monotonic and preserves ranking, while isotonic can alter AUC via ties; beta calibration is a useful parametric alternative when score distortion is skewed.
implementation_hint: Train 3 families on identical race-held-out folds: current GBDT classifier, ranking model, and hazard model. Blend only from out-of-fold predictions, then calibrate the final blend with sigmoid first; test beta calibration as a second-stage option only if logloss/Brier improves without hurting AUC on fold holdouts.
expected_effect: Usually the safest path to incremental leaderboard gain once single-model feature work saturates.
risk: If base models share the same leakage or fold design, the ensemble just amplifies CV optimism. Isotonic on the final blend can hurt AUC.
